## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

28250cb1  ₢BBAAQ    Surface 1 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: BUS0 multi-element fact-file convention authored against BBAAO landed code (commit bffc58cb). Four new quoins enrolled in mapping section and bodied in §"Fact-File Pattern", arranged as concept/impl/instance triples paralleling the existing single-form set. (1) :busff_multi_fact: — concept quoin sibling to busff_fact_file. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body composes three rules: type-as-extension naming (<root>.<extension>; root identifies per-row instance, extension classifies fact type), closed registry (each allowed extension is a BUF_EXT_<NAME> constant in buf_fact.sh; constant value matches name downcased; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape), BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions; RB-domain extensions register in rbgc_Constants.sh under RBGP_FACT_EXT_*; neither universe enumerates the other; boundary by producer-module ownership not on-disk location). Emission cardinality stated explicitly: one write per <root,extension> key per dispatch — second write is a bug surfaced fatally by buf_write_fact_multi's preexistence check. Distinguishing prose pinned at end: single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (extension is the contract, root is per-row). (2) :buf_write_fact_multi: — implementation quoin sibling to buf_write_fact_single. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body: signature buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>, dual-write to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR + BURD_TEMP_DIR mirroring single-form, fail-on-preexist, ext is opaque pass-through (registered BUF_EXT_* constant at call site is the contract, no in-function registry validation — out-of-registry usage is producer-side bug for code review). Code example shows per-row emission inside a producer loop using BUF_EXT_ALIAS. Cross-refs busff_multi_fact for the implemented pattern and buf_write_fact_single for the mirrored shape. (3) :buf_ext_alias: — instance quoin sibling to buf_burx_env. Voicing axl_voices axt_enum_value (closed-registry member shape; intentional mismatch with buf_burx_env's axpof_fact precedent which voices a fact-file filename rather than a registry-member token). Body documents BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias" as registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURH alias observation facts; written by burh_list_capture per discovered alias profile with empty content (presence-of-file is the fact); flagged as first-and-sole registry member serving as proof-of-shape for the convention. (4) Dependency-required rename bundled in same commit (out-of-Surface-1 staleness from BBAAO that Surface 1 text cannot ignore): :buf_write_fact: → :buf_write_fact_single: at mapping line, anchor, all body references including code examples, and one downstream reference inside burx_regime body. Body language updated to pair cleanly with the new multi-form ("All single-form fact-file producers must use buf_write_fact_single ... multi-row producers use buf_write_fact_multi"). Code example comment for the burx.env case rephrased "Single-slot fact whose body has structured content" to disambiguate from the new multi-fact terminology. Section structure post-edit reads as concept-then-impl pairs: busff_fact_file → busff_multi_fact → buf_write_fact_single → buf_write_fact_multi → buf_burx_env → buf_ext_alias. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched per docket scope: README glossary entries for the multi-fact convention sit in Surface 3 of this pace; existing buf_burx_env voicing predates linked-term sweeps and uses bare //axpof_fact (no axl_voices) — not load-bearing for Surface 1; sibling specs that may reference buf_write_fact bare-name unswept. (1) RBS0 mapping section drops three stale entries — rbrr_depot_project_id, rbrr_gar_repository, rbrr_gcb_pool_stem — matching BBAAM Step 2's precedent of deleting RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID from mapping when its bash constant was dropped from rbgc_Constants.sh. Four new quoins enrolled in mapping section and bodied in §"Fact-File Pattern", arranged as concept/impl/instance triples paralleling the existing single-form set. (1) :busff_multi_fact: — concept quoin sibling to busff_fact_file. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body composes three rules: type-as-extension naming (<root>.<extension>; root identifies per-row instance, extension classifies fact type), closed registry (each allowed extension is a BUF_EXT_<NAME> constant in buf_fact.sh; constant value matches name downcased; opaque ad-hoc extensions are not the shape), BUK/RB boundary discipline (BUK registry holds only BUK-domain extensions; RB-domain extensions register in rbgc_Constants.sh under RBGP_FACT_EXT_*; neither universe enumerates the other; boundary by producer-module ownership not on-disk location). Emission cardinality stated explicitly: one write per <root,extension> key per dispatch — second write is a bug surfaced fatally by buf_write_fact_multi's preexistence check. Distinguishing prose pinned at end: single-form is one named-content slot per dispatch (filename is the contract); multi-form is many same-shaped emissions per dispatch (extension is the contract, root is per-row). (2) :buf_write_fact_multi: — implementation quoin sibling to buf_write_fact_single. Voicing axl_voices axo_entity. Body: signature buf_write_fact_multi <file_root> <ext> <value>, dual-write to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR + BURD_TEMP_DIR mirroring single-form, fail-on-preexist, ext is opaque pass-through (registered BUF_EXT_* constant at call site is the contract, no in-function registry validation — out-of-registry usage is producer-side bug for code review). Code example shows per-row emission inside a producer loop using BUF_EXT_ALIAS. Cross-refs busff_multi_fact for the implemented pattern and buf_write_fact_single for the mirrored shape. (3) :buf_ext_alias: — instance quoin sibling to buf_burx_env. Voicing axl_voices axt_enum_value (closed-registry member shape; intentional mismatch with buf_burx_env's axpof_fact precedent which voices a fact-file filename rather than a registry-member token). Body documents BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias" as registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURH alias observation facts; written by burh_list_capture per discovered alias profile with empty content (presence-of-file is the fact); flagged as first-and-sole registry member serving as proof-of-shape for the convention. (4) Dependency-required rename bundled in same commit (out-of-Surface-1 staleness from BBAAO that Surface 1 text cannot ignore): :buf_write_fact: → :buf_write_fact_single: at mapping line, anchor, all body references including code examples, and one downstream reference inside burx_regime body. Body language updated to pair cleanly with the new multi-form ("All single-form fact-file producers must use buf_write_fact_single ... multi-row producers use buf_write_fact_multi"). Code example comment for the burx.env case rephrased "Single-slot fact whose body has structured content" to disambiguate from the new multi-fact terminology. Section structure post-edit reads as concept-then-impl pairs: busff_fact_file → busff_multi_fact → buf_write_fact_single → buf_write_fact_multi → buf_burx_env → buf_ext_alias. Out-of-surface staleness surfaced but not touched per docket scope: README glossary entries for the multi-fact convention sit in Surface 3 of this pace; existing buf_burx_env voicing predates linked-term sweeps and uses bare //axpof_fact (no axl_voices) — not load-bearing for Surface 1; sibling specs that may reference buf_write_fact bare-name unswept. a67aefea  ₢BBAAQ    Surface 4 of multi-fact-cult-verb-spec: RBS0 + RBSDE depot-identity banner sweep matching BBAAM Steps 1-5 code reality. (1) RBS0 mapping section drops three stale entries — rbrr_depot_project_id, rbrr_gar_repository, rbrr_gcb_pool_stem — matching BBAAM Step 2's precedent of deleting RBGP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID from mapping when its bash constant was dropped from rbgc_Constants.sh. The three RBRR fields were similarly dropped from rbrr_regime.sh enrollment in BBAAM Step 1 but their RBS0 mapping/definitions were missed in that sweep. (2) RBS0 definition blocks deleted at the corresponding [[anchor]] sites: rbrr_depot_project_id (former lines 2610-2617), rbrr_gar_repository (former 2629-2640 — its body referenced rbrr_depot_project_id, gone with parent), rbrr_gcb_pool_stem (former 2731-2740). The standalone NOTE block on private-pool requirement that followed gcb_pool_stem (machine type naming systems, regional pricing, e2/n2d/c3 family validation) preserved in place since it ends with a binding constraint on rbrr_gcb_machine_type — load-bearing content for that surviving quoin. (3) RBS0 line 1833 prose reference to RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM rephrased to '(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)' derivation in the vouch-on-private-pool description. (4) RBSDE rewritten to match BBAAM Step 2 zero-arg, RBDC-driven shape: dropped axhopt_typed_parameter section taking «INPUT_DEPOT_NAME» (no CLI param now), dropped timestamp-generation step (timestamp eliminated by collapse), added 'Derive Depot Identity' axhos_step exposing the four RBDC values «DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»=${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}, «GAR_REPOSITORY»=${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar, «WORKER_POOL_STEM»=${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool, «BUILD_BUCKET»=${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}. Resource construction sites updated to reference the derived constants by name. Mason SA naming preserved as mason-«RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER» per paddock's Mason-SA-name-unchanged lock. These sit outside the docket's RBS0 + RBSDE surface and would need a separate sweep pace. The buym→buh missing-kindle bug class is closed; previous cycle's diagnosis fully discharged. Surfaced this cycle: rblm_cli.sh:226 dies with 'RBZ_ONBOARDING: unbound variable' on the post-success 'Next:' display hint that pointers operators at the onboarding tabtarget after marshal-zero completes. Root cause: typo at the consumer site — code reaches for the constant family's intuitive name (RBZ_ONBOARDING) but the registered name is RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE (zipper enrollment at rbz_zipper.sh:85, points at rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh). Constant defined exactly once, referenced at exactly one site (this typo'd consumer); grep for RBZ_ONBOARDING returns only the broken site. Fix: one-line rename at rblm_cli.sh:226, RBZ_ONBOARDING → RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE. The mutation+commit body of marshal-zero (lines 1-222) was untouched — bug is post-success cosmetic display routing, but its non-zero exit breaks the fail-fast caller contract that rbw-tP will consume. In-scope-additive to ₢BBAAM per cycle-driven-debug docket disposition: in-scope-blocking the verification path (same shape as last cycle's four-CLI sweep — surfaced during verification, routes into BBAAM rather than reopening A6 or any other upstream pace). Build/test verification deferred to next cycle's rbw-MZ rerun (matching the previous cycle's notch pattern of deferring confirmation to the subsequent cycle). Probably wants tty-detection auto-skip with a clearer error pointing at the env var, OR an explicit BURE_CONFIRM_TTY_REQUIRED contract; not in-scope for this pace. (2) Operator-prerequisite chain check ('HEAD has unpushed commits') fired correctly on first attempt — guard worked, surfaced via clean error. Verification path next reachable step: re-run rbw-MZ to confirm the RBZ_ONBOARDING rename closes this cycle's surface, then advance to pristine-lifecycle fixture (cases 1, 2, 3). .claude/jjm/officia/260427-1018/gazette_out.md
.claude/jjm/officia/260423-1013/gazette_out.md

.claude/jjm/officia/260426-1028/gazette_out.md

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Operator scope
55	
56	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
57	
58	## Coupling to ₣A_
59	
60	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
61	
62	## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)
63	
64	These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
65	
66	**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.
67	
68	**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.
69	
70	**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:
71	
72	- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
73	- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
74	- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
75	- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`
76	
77	**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.
78	
79	**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.
80	
81	**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.
82	
83	**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).
84	
85	## References
86	
87	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
88	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
89	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
90	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
91	
92	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAT
93	
94	## Character
95	
96	Mechanical correctness sweep on BBAAS. Call sites settled; no design.
97	
98	## Scope
99	
100	- Drop `billingEnabled=true` filter in `zrbgp_depot_state_emit` (hides DELETE_REQUESTED unnecessarily).
101	- Reorder: CRM lifecycle lookup before IAM SA list.
102	- Add `rbgu_http_code_capture` check on CRM lookup; `|| true` + jq fallback is silently lossy.
103	- Add `nextPageToken` pagination on Billing v1 projects list and IAM v1 serviceAccounts list.
104	- Delete orphan `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN` constant.
105	
106	Empirical check after: does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects? If no, surface graveyard-observability as a separate pace.
107	
108	## References
109	
110	- BBAAS notch 4d810db7
111	- BBAAM paused; resumes after this pace closes
112

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAT

## Character

Mechanical correctness sweep on BBAAS. Call sites settled; no design.

## Scope

- Drop `billingEnabled=true` filter in `zrbgp_depot_state_emit` (hides DELETE_REQUESTED unnecessarily).
- Reorder: CRM lifecycle lookup before IAM SA list.
- Add `rbgu_http_code_capture` check on CRM lookup; `|| true` + jq fallback is silently lossy.
- Add `nextPageToken` pagination on Billing v1 projects list and IAM v1 serviceAccounts list.
- Delete orphan `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN` constant.

Empirical check after: does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects? If no, surface graveyard-observability as a separate pace.

## References

- BBAAS notch 4d810db7
- BBAAM paused; resumes after this pace closes

140	  echo "${z_access_token}"
141	}
142	
143	# Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
144	# Classification is by Mason SA presence — any billing-account project hosting
145	# a service account matching ^mason-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@ is a depot.  The moniker
146	# is derived from the Mason SA local-part after stripping the mason- prefix.
147	# DELETE_REQUESTED projects are recorded as such.  Per-moniker fact files
148	# named "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" (state) and
149	# "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}" (project_id) are written via
150	# buf_write_fact_multi; consumers walk the emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR /
151	# BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
152	#
153	# RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN is never emitted — Mason SA presence IS the
154	# discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are silently skipped.
155	#
156	# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist.
157	#
158	# Args: <oauth_access_token>
159	zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
160	  zrbgp_sentinel
161	
162	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
163	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbgp_depot_state_emit: token required"
164	
165	  # RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is min=0 in the regime (optional for payor-only
166	  # operations) but required here to enumerate depot projects.
167	  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" \
168	    || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set — required for depot_list"
169	
170	  # Query Cloud Billing API v1: list all projects under the billing account.
171	  local -r z_billing_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects"
172	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_billing_list_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_state_billing"
173	  rbgu_http_require_ok "List billing account projects" "depot_state_billing"
174	
175	  local z_project_count
176	  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" '.projectBillingInfo // [] | length') \
177	    || z_project_count=0
178	
179	  test "${z_project_count}" -gt 0 || return 0
180	
181	  # Per-iteration synthesized locals (BCG exception 2 — declare outside, assign inside)
182	  local z_index=0
183	  local z_project_id=""
184	  local z_billing_enabled=""
185	  local z_sa_list_infix=""
186	  local z_sa_list_code=""
187	  local z_sa_count=""
188	  local z_sa_index=0
189	  local z_sa_email=""
190	  local z_mason_email=""
191	  local z_mason_local=""
192	  local z_moniker=""
193	  local z_crm_infix=""
194	  local z_lifecycle_state=""
195	  local z_state=""
196	
197	  while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
198	    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].projectId") \
199	      || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }
200	
201	    z_billing_enabled=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].billingEnabled") \
202	      || z_billing_enabled="false"
203	
204	    if test "${z_billing_enabled}" != "true"; then
205	      buc_log_args "Skipping project with billing disabled: ${z_project_id}"
206	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
207	      continue
208	    fi
209	
210	    # List service accounts in this project; treat HTTP errors as skip (not fatal).
211	    z_sa_list_infix="depot_state_sa_${z_index}"
212	    rbgu_http_json "GET" \
213	      "${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts" \
214	      "${z_token}" "${z_sa_list_infix}" || true
215	
216	    z_sa_list_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}") || z_sa_list_code=""
217	    if test "${z_sa_list_code}" != "200"; then
218	      buc_log_args "Skipping project (SA list HTTP ${z_sa_list_code}): ${z_project_id}"
219	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
220	      continue
221	    fi
222	
223	    z_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
224	      || z_sa_count=0
225	
226	    # Scan SA list for a Mason SA — moniker portion enforced to RBRR's
227	    # ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape so an unrelated SA literally named e.g.
228	    # "mason-Foo" cannot be misclassified as a depot.
229	    z_mason_email=""
230	    z_sa_index=0
231	    while test "${z_sa_index}" -lt "${z_sa_count}"; do
232	      z_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}" ".accounts[${z_sa_index}].email") \
233	        || { z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1)); continue; }
234	      if [[ "${z_sa_email}" =~ ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@[^@]*\.iam\.gserviceaccount\.com$ ]]; then
235	        z_mason_email="${z_sa_email}"
236	        break
237	      fi
238	      z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1))
239	    done
240	
241	    if test -z "${z_mason_email}"; then
242	      buc_log_args "Skipping project — no Mason SA found: ${z_project_id}"
243	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
244	      continue
245	    fi
246	
247	    # Derive moniker from Mason SA local-part (strip leading mason- prefix)
248	    z_mason_local="${z_mason_email%%@*}"
249	    z_moniker="${z_mason_local#"${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-"}"
250	
251	    # Determine lifecycle state via CRM v1.
252	    z_crm_infix="depot_state_crm_${z_index}"
253	    rbgu_http_json "GET" \
254	      "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}" \
255	      "${z_token}" "${z_crm_infix}" || true
256	
257	    z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_crm_infix}" '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"') \
258	      || z_lifecycle_state="UNKNOWN"
259	
260	    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
261	      z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
262	    elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
263	      z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
264	    else
265	      buc_log_args "Skipping moniker ${z_moniker} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
266	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
267	      continue
268	    fi
269	
270	    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" "${z_state}"
271	    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}" "${z_project_id}"
272	
273	    z_index=$((z_index + 1))
274	  done
275	}
276	
277	# Post-lifecycle hook: refresh per-moniker depot fact files.
278	# Called by levy and unmake at end-of-operation so observers (theurge
279	# autodetect, manual inspection) see fresh state.
280	zrbgp_depot_list_update() {
281	  zrbgp_sentinel
282	
283	  buc_log_args "Refreshing per-moniker depot fact files"
284	
285	  local z_token
286	  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate for depot fact refresh"
287	
288	  zrbgp_depot_state_emit "${z_token}"
289	}
290	
291	######################################################################
292	# External Functions (rbgp_*)
293	
294	zrbgp_billing_attach() {
295	  zrbgp_sentinel
296	
297	  local -r z_billing_account="${1:-}"
298	
299	  buc_doc_brief "Attach a billing account to the project"
300	  buc_doc_param "billing_account" "Billing account ID to attach"
301	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
302	
303	  test -n "${z_billing_account}" || buc_die "Billing account ID required"
304	
305	  buc_step "Attaching billing account: ${z_billing_account}"
306	
307	  local z_token
308	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
309	  local -r z_billing_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_attach.json"
310	  jq -n --arg billingAccountName "billingAccounts/${z_billing_account}" \
311	    --arg projectId "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" \
312	    '{
313	      billingAccountName: $billingAccountName,
314	      projectId: $projectId,
315	      billingEnabled: true
316	    }' > "${z_billing_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing attach body"
317	
318	  local -r z_billing_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}:setBillingInfo"
319	  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" \
320	                                  "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH}" "${z_billing_body}"
321	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Attach billing account" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH}"
322	
323	  buc_success "Billing account ${z_billing_account} attached to project"
324	}
325	
326	zrbgp_billing_detach() {
327	  zrbgp_sentinel
328	
329	  buc_doc_brief "Detach billing account from the project"
330	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
331	
332	  buc_step "Detaching billing account from project"
333	
334	  local z_token
335	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
336	  local -r z_billing_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_detach.json"
337	  jq -n --arg projectId "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" \
338	    '{
339	      projectId: $projectId,
340	      billingEnabled: false
341	    }' > "${z_billing_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing detach body"
342	
343	  local -r z_billing_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}:setBillingInfo"
344	  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" \
345	                                  "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_DETACH}" "${z_billing_body}"
346	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Detach billing account" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_DETACH}"
347	
348	  buc_success "Billing account detached from project"
349	}
350	
351	
352	
353	zrbgp_liens_list() {
354	  zrbgp_sentinel
355	
356	  buc_doc_brief "List all liens on the project"
357	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
358	
359	  buc_step "Listing liens on project: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
360	
361	  local z_token
362	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
363	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens?parent=projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}"
364	  rbgu_http_require_ok "List liens" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}"
365	
366	  local z_lien_count
367	  z_lien_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}" '.liens // [] | length') || buc_die "Failed to parse liens response"
368	
369	  if test "${z_lien_count}" -eq 0; then
370	    buc_info "No liens found on project"
371	    return 0
372	  fi
373	
374	  buc_step "Found ${z_lien_count} lien(s):"
375	  jq -r '.liens[]? | "  - " + .name + " (reason: " + .reason + ")"' \
376	    "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}" || true
377	
378	  return 0
379	}
380	
381	zrbgp_lien_delete() {
382	  zrbgp_sentinel
383	
384	  local -r z_lien_name="${1:-}"
385	
386	  buc_doc_brief "Delete a specific lien from the project"
387	  buc_doc_param "lien_name" "Full resource name of the lien to delete"
388	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
389	
390	  test -n "${z_lien_name}" || buc_die "Lien name required"
391	
392	  buc_step "Deleting lien: ${z_lien_name}"
393	
394	  local z_token
395	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
396	  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${RBGC_API_CRM_DELETE_LIEN}/${z_lien_name}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_DELETE_LIEN}"
397	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete lien" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_DELETE_LIEN}" 404 "not found (already deleted)"
398	
399	  buc_success "Lien deleted: ${z_lien_name}"
400	}
401	
402	
403	######################################################################
404	# Capture: list required services that are NOT enabled (blank = all enabled)
405	zrbgp_required_apis_missing_capture() {
406	  zrbgp_sentinel
407	
408	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
409	  test -n "${z_token}" || { echo ""; return 1; }
410	
411	  local z_missing=""
412	  local z_api=""
413	  local z_service=""
414	  local z_infix=""
415	  local z_state=""
416	  local z_code=""
417	
418	  for z_api in                       \
419	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_CRM}"      \
420	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_GAR}"      \
421	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_IAM}"      \
422	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_BUILD}"    \
423	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_ANALYSIS}" \
424	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_STORAGE}"
425	  do
426	    z_service="${z_api##*/}"
427	    z_infix="${ZRBGP_INFIX_API_CHECK}_${z_service}"
428	
429	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_api}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" || true
430	
431	    buc_log_args 'If we cannot even read an HTTP code file, that is a processing failure.'
432	    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") || z_code=""
433	    test -n "${z_code}" || return 1
434	
435	    if test "${z_code}" = "200"; then
436	      z_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".state") || z_state=""
437	      test "${z_state}" = "ENABLED" || z_missing="${z_missing} ${z_service}"
438	    else
439	      buc_log_args 'Any non-200 (403/404/5xx/etc) => treat as NOT enabled'

file.content:
echo "${z_access_token}"
}

# Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
# Classification is by Mason SA presence — any billing-account project hosting
# a service account matching ^mason-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@ is a depot.  The moniker
# is derived from the Mason SA local-part after stripping the mason- prefix.
# DELETE_REQUESTED projects are recorded as such.  Per-moniker fact files
# named "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" (state) and
# "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}" (project_id) are written via
# buf_write_fact_multi; consumers walk the emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR /
# BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
#
# RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN is never emitted — Mason SA presence IS the
# discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are silently skipped.
#
# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist.
#
# Args: <oauth_access_token>
zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbgp_depot_state_emit: token required"

  # RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is min=0 in the regime (optional for payor-only
  # operations) but required here to enumerate depot projects.
  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" \
    || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set — required for depot_list"

  # Query Cloud Billing API v1: list all projects under the billing account.
  local -r z_billing_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_billing_list_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_state_billing"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List billing account projects" "depot_state_billing"

  local z_project_count
  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" '.projectBillingInfo // [] | length') \
    || z_project_count=0

  test "${z_project_count}" -gt 0 || return 0

  # Per-iteration synthesized locals (BCG exception 2 — declare outside, assign inside)
  local z_index=0
  local z_project_id=""
  local z_billing_enabled=""
  local z_sa_list_infix=""
  local z_sa_list_code=""
  local z_sa_count=""
  local z_sa_index=0
  local z_sa_email=""
  local z_mason_email=""
  local z_mason_local=""
  local z_moniker=""
  local z_crm_infix=""
  local z_lifecycle_state=""
  local z_state=""

  while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].projectId") \
      || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }

    z_billing_enabled=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].billingEnabled") \
      || z_billing_enabled="false"

    if test "${z_billing_enabled}" != "true"; then
      buc_log_args "Skipping project with billing disabled: ${z_project_id}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    # List service accounts in this project; treat HTTP errors as skip (not fatal).
    z_sa_list_infix="depot_state_sa_${z_index}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" \
      "${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts" \
      "${z_token}" "${z_sa_list_infix}" || true

    z_sa_list_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}") || z_sa_list_code=""
    if test "${z_sa_list_code}" != "200"; then
      buc_log_args "Skipping project (SA list HTTP ${z_sa_list_code}): ${z_project_id}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    z_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
      || z_sa_count=0

    # Scan SA list for a Mason SA — moniker portion enforced to RBRR's
    # ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape so an unrelated SA literally named e.g.
    # "mason-Foo" cannot be misclassified as a depot.
    z_mason_email=""
    z_sa_index=0
    while test "${z_sa_index}" -lt "${z_sa_count}"; do
      z_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}" ".accounts[${z_sa_index}].email") \
        || { z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1)); continue; }
      if [[ "${z_sa_email}" =~ ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@[^@]*\.iam\.gserviceaccount\.com$ ]]; then
        z_mason_email="${z_sa_email}"
        break
      fi
      z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1))
    done

    if test -z "${z_mason_email}"; then
      buc_log_args "Skipping project — no Mason SA found: ${z_project_id}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    # Derive moniker from Mason SA local-part (strip leading mason- prefix)
    z_mason_local="${z_mason_email%%@*}"
    z_moniker="${z_mason_local#"${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-"}"

    # Determine lifecycle state via CRM v1.
    z_crm_infix="depot_state_crm_${z_index}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" \
      "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}" \
      "${z_token}" "${z_crm_infix}" || true

    z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_crm_infix}" '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"') \
      || z_lifecycle_state="UNKNOWN"

    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
      z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
    elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
      z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
    else
      buc_log_args "Skipping moniker ${z_moniker} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" "${z_state}"
    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}" "${z_project_id}"

    z_index=$((z_index + 1))
  done
}

# Post-lifecycle hook: refresh per-moniker depot fact files.
# Called by levy and unmake at end-of-operation so observers (theurge
# autodetect, manual inspection) see fresh state.
zrbgp_depot_list_update() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_log_args "Refreshing per-moniker depot fact files"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) || buc_die "Failed to authenticate for depot fact refresh"

  zrbgp_depot_state_emit "${z_token}"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbgp_*)

zrbgp_billing_attach() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_billing_account="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Attach a billing account to the project"
  buc_doc_param "billing_account" "Billing account ID to attach"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_billing_account}" || buc_die "Billing account ID required"

  buc_step "Attaching billing account: ${z_billing_account}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
  local -r z_billing_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_attach.json"
  jq -n --arg billingAccountName "billingAccounts/${z_billing_account}" \
    --arg projectId "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" \
    '{
      billingAccountName: $billingAccountName,
      projectId: $projectId,
      billingEnabled: true
    }' > "${z_billing_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing attach body"

  local -r z_billing_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}:setBillingInfo"
  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" \
                                  "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH}" "${z_billing_body}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Attach billing account" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH}"

  buc_success "Billing account ${z_billing_account} attached to project"
}

zrbgp_billing_detach() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Detach billing account from the project"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Detaching billing account from project"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
  local -r z_billing_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_billing_detach.json"
  jq -n --arg projectId "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" \
    '{
      projectId: $projectId,
      billingEnabled: false
    }' > "${z_billing_body}" || buc_die "Failed to build billing detach body"

  local -r z_billing_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}:setBillingInfo"
  rbgu_http_json "PUT" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" \
                                  "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_DETACH}" "${z_billing_body}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Detach billing account" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_DETACH}"

  buc_success "Billing account detached from project"
}



zrbgp_liens_list() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "List all liens on the project"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Listing liens on project: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens?parent=projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List liens" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}"

  local z_lien_count
  z_lien_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}" '.liens // [] | length') || buc_die "Failed to parse liens response"

  if test "${z_lien_count}" -eq 0; then
    buc_info "No liens found on project"
    return 0
  fi

  buc_step "Found ${z_lien_count} lien(s):"
  jq -r '.liens[]? | "  - " + .name + " (reason: " + .reason + ")"' \
    "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}" || true

  return 0
}

zrbgp_lien_delete() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_lien_name="${1:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Delete a specific lien from the project"
  buc_doc_param "lien_name" "Full resource name of the lien to delete"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_lien_name}" || buc_die "Lien name required"

  buc_step "Deleting lien: ${z_lien_name}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${RBGC_API_CRM_DELETE_LIEN}/${z_lien_name}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_DELETE_LIEN}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete lien" "${ZRBGP_INFIX_DELETE_LIEN}" 404 "not found (already deleted)"

  buc_success "Lien deleted: ${z_lien_name}"
}


######################################################################
# Capture: list required services that are NOT enabled (blank = all enabled)
zrbgp_required_apis_missing_capture() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || { echo ""; return 1; }

  local z_missing=""
  local z_api=""
  local z_service=""
  local z_infix=""
  local z_state=""
  local z_code=""

  for z_api in                       \
    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_CRM}"      \
    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_GAR}"      \
    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_IAM}"      \
    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_BUILD}"    \
    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_ANALYSIS}" \
    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_STORAGE}"
  do
    z_service="${z_api##*/}"
    z_infix="${ZRBGP_INFIX_API_CHECK}_${z_service}"

    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_api}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" || true

    buc_log_args 'If we cannot even read an HTTP code file, that is a processing failure.'
    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") || z_code=""
    test -n "${z_code}" || return 1

    if test "${z_code}" = "200"; then
      z_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".state") || z_state=""
      test "${z_state}" = "ENABLED" || z_missing="${z_missing} ${z_service}"
    else
      buc_log_args 'Any non-200 (403/404/5xx/etc) => treat as NOT enabled'

content.text:
## Mounted ₣BB → ₢BBAAT

**Heat:** rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (8/19 done, racing)

**Paddock summary:** ₣BB introduces `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third release-qualification tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The recent ₢BBAAS pace reshaped `rbgp_depot_list` to use Mason SA presence as the depot signature (Cloud Billing v1 → IAM SA list → Mason classifier → CRM lifecycle → dual fact files). BBAAS landed at 4d810db7; verification (BBAAM) is paused awaiting this correctness sweep.

**Recent work:** BBAAQ Surfaces 1, 2, 4 (multi-fact + cult-verb spec authoring); BBAAS landed the depot_list reshape; BBAAM cycle-driven debug paused.

**Pace ₢BBAAT (rough):** depot-list-correctness-sweep — silks fits docket cleanly, no rename needed.

**Surfaced surfaces against the BBAAS code:**
1. `rbgp_Payor.sh:201-208` — `billingEnabled` filter skips DELETE_REQUESTED projects (billing auto-disables on delete request)
2. `rbgp_Payor.sh:210-221` (IAM SA list) currently runs **before** lines 251-258 (CRM lifecycle) — docket asks to swap
3. `rbgp_Payor.sh:251-258` (CRM lookup) uses `|| true` + jq `.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"` fallback — silently swallows HTTP errors
4. Billing v1 list (`rbgp_Payor.sh:171-179`) and IAM SA list (212-214) — neither paginates `nextPageToken`
5. `rbgc_Constants.sh:159` — `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN="BROKEN"` orphan; one comment ref at `rbgp_Payor.sh:153` will need to follow

**Approach (mechanical, no design):**
- Edit `rbgp_Payor.sh` `zrbgp_depot_state_emit` in place: drop billing filter, swap order so CRM-first → branch on lifecycle → only continue to SA list for ACTIVE (skipping IAM call entirely on DELETE_REQUESTED answers the empirical question by construction), add `rbgu_http_code_capture` check on CRM, wrap both Billing-list and SA-list in `nextPageToken` loops.
- Delete `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN` from `rbgc_Constants.sh`; update the BBAAS-era comment at `rbgp_Payor.sh:153`.
- Verify: `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` + `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` (the 3 known post-marshal-zero failures from BBAAS will persist; not regressions).

**One concern worth raising before I start:** the docket frames the empirical check ("does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects?") as a follow-up. But if I do the CRM-first reorder and short-circuit on DELETE_REQUESTED before listing SAs, I avoid asking that question — DELETE_REQUESTED projects emit no moniker. That means we'd skip-emit DELETE_REQUESTED projects entirely (since moniker derives from Mason SA local-part). That contradicts scope item #1's framing ("hides DELETE_REQUESTED unnecessarily"). So either:
- (a) **CRM-first short-circuit** drops DELETE_REQUESTED observability — wrong for the stated intent
- (b) **CRM-first + still list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED** — keeps empirical check live, costs an IAM call per dead project
- (c) **Reorder means something else** — e.g., move CRM up so we check lifecycle of the project we're about to label, but still list SAs unconditionally

Reading the docket again: "Empirical check after: does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects?" suggests (b) — keep listing, observe behavior. (c) is plausible too.

**Execution strategy:** Sequential, single-file. Tier: this opus session already loaded; mechanical enough that sonnet would handle it if you'd rather hand off. ~10-15 minutes including verification.

Ready to proceed once you resolve the (a)/(b)/(c) ambiguity — which reorder shape did you mean?

message.content: Help me decide what is simplest, most coherent, most legible approach given your concern options?

content.text:
The docket's empirical check ("does IAM list SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED projects?") only stays live if we **don't** short-circuit on lifecycle. That alone rules out (a). Between (b) and (c):

**(b) is simplest.** One linear loop body with two gates in conceptual order:

```
for each project from Billing list (paginated):
  CRM lookup + rbgu_http_code_capture check
    non-200 → log + skip (observability event, not silent)
    lifecycleState ∉ {ACTIVE, DELETE_REQUESTED} → log + skip
  SA list (paginated) → find Mason SA → derive moniker
    no Mason SA → log + skip
  emit moniker.depot=state, moniker.depot-project=project_id
```

**Why (b) over (c):**

- **Coherent failure mode per step.** CRM has its own HTTP code check (matches the existing SA-list code-check pattern at the current 216-221). SA list has its Mason-presence check. Each gate decides one thing.
- **Conceptual order matches mental model.** "Is the project alive-ish? Does it have a Mason SA? If both, it's a depot." Current order asks "is there a Mason SA?" before "is the project alive?" — reads backward.
- **Drops the billing filter cleanly.** With CRM-first, billing-disabled live projects (rare but possible) flow through to lifecycle classification rather than getting dropped on a stale property.
- **CRM is cheaper than IAM SA list** — gating on the cheaper call is the conventional shape.
- **Empirical question stays observable.** A DELETE_REQUESTED project with a Mason SA emits its moniker; a DELETE_REQUESTED project where IAM returns empty SAs gets the "no Mason SA" log. Both outcomes are visible in operator output. If the empirical answer turns out to be "IAM hides SAs on DELETE_REQUESTED," that surfaces as a separate graveyard-observability pace, exactly per docket.

**(c) is a strictly weaker variant of (b)** — it preserves SA-list-first while still moving CRM "before labeling." That keeps the backward conceptual order, costs an IAM call before we know lifecycle, and gives you no expressive benefit. The docket's literal "CRM before IAM SA list" reads as (b).

Recommend (b). Want me to proceed?
Let me check for existing pagination helpers and the URL builder.

local z_code
  local z_err
  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_GCS_BUCKET_CREATE}" "${z_token}" \
                                  "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}" "${z_bucket_req}"
  z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}") || buc_die "Bad bucket creation HTTP code"
  z_err=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}" '.error.message') || z_err="HTTP ${z_code}"

  case "${z_code}" in
    200|201) buc_info "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} created";                    return 0 ;;
    409)     buc_die  "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} already exists (pristine-state violation)" ;;
    *)       buc_die  "Failed to create bucket: ${z_err}"                             ;;
  esac
}

zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_token="${1}"
  local z_bucket_name="${2}"

  local z_list_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b/${z_bucket_name}/o"
  local z_page_token=""
  local z_first=1

  while :; do
    buc_log_args "Build URL with optional pageToken -> ${z_first}"
    local z_url="${z_list_url_base}"
    if test -n "${z_page_token}"; then
      buc_log_args 'pageToken must be URL-encoded'
      local z_tok_enc
      z_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_page_token}") || return 1
      z_url="${z_url}?pageToken=${z_tok_enc}"
    fi

    buc_log_args 'Use a unique infix per page to avoid clobbering files'
    local z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_LIST}${z_first}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}"

    local z_code
    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") || return 1
    test "${z_code}" = "200" || return 1

    buc_log_args 'Print names from this page (if any)'
    buc_log_args 'Next page?'
    jq -r                '.items[]?.name // empty' "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"  || return 1
    z_page_token=$(jq -r '.nextPageToken // empty' "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}") || return 1

    test -n "${z_page_token}" || break
    z_first=$((z_first + 1))
  done
}

zrbgg_get_project_number_capture() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || return 1

  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_CRM_GET_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Get project info"                         "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO}" || return 1

  local z_project_number
  z_project_number=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO}" '.projectNumber') || return 1
  test -n "${z_project_number}" || return 1

  echo "${z_project_number}"
}

zrbgg_empty_gcs_bucket() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at

#

# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GCP Payor - Billing and Destructive Lifecycle Operations

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBGP_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgp multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGP_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgp_*)

zrbgp_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGP_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgp already kindled"

  buc_log_args "Ensure dependencies are kindled first"
  zrbgc_sentinel
  zrbgo_sentinel
  zrbgu_sentinel
  zrbgi_sentinel

  readonly ZRBGP_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_"
  readonly ZRBGP_EMPTY_JSON="${ZRBGP_PREFIX}empty.json"
  printf '{}' > "${ZRBGP_EMPTY_JSON}"

  readonly ZRBGP_SCRATCH_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgp_scratch.txt"

  # Infix values for HTTP operations
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE="project_delete"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE="project_restore"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE="project_state"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_LIST_LIENS="list_liens"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_DELETE_LIEN="delete_lien"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_ATTACH="billing_attach"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_BILLING_DETACH="billing_detach"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_CREATE_REPO="create_repo"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_VERIFY_REPO="verify_repo"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO="project_info"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE="bucket_create"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_API_CHECK="api_checking"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_LIST_SA="gov_list_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_DELETE_SA="gov_delete_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_CREATE_SA="gov_create_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_VERIFY_SA="gov_verify_sa"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_KEY="gov_key"
  readonly ZRBGP_INFIX_GOV_IAM="gov_iam"

  readonly ZRBGP_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgp_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGP_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgp not kindled - call zrbgp_kindle first"
}


######################################################################
# OAuth Authentication Functions (zrbgp_oauth_*)

zrbgp_refresh_capture() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  # RBRO credentials already loaded and validated by caller (rbgu_rbro_load)
  zrbro_sentinel
  test -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID not set in environment"

  buc_log_args "Exchanging refresh token for access token"

  # Build request and pipe to curl - secrets never touch disk
  local z_response
  z_response=$(jq -n \
    --arg refresh_token "${RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN}" \
    --arg client_id "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}" \
    --arg client_secret "${RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET}" \

  zrbgu_sentinel
  local z_s="${1:-}"

rbgu_json_field_capture() {
  zrbgu_sentinel
  local -r z_infix="${1}"
  local -r z_jq="${2}"
  local -r z_json_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"
  local z_result
  z_result=$(jq -r "${z_jq}" "${z_json_file}")          || return 1
  if test -z "${z_result}" || test "${z_result}" = "null"; then return 1; fi
  echo "${z_result}"
}

  zrbgu_sentinel
  local -r z_infix="${1}"
  local -r z_file="${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_CODE}"
  local z_code
  z_code=$(<"${z_file}") || return 1
  test -n "${z_code}"    || return 1
  echo "${z_code}"
}

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE}") || return 1

input.old_string:
# Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
# Classification is by Mason SA presence — any billing-account project hosting
# a service account matching ^mason-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@ is a depot.  The moniker
# is derived from the Mason SA local-part after stripping the mason- prefix.
# DELETE_REQUESTED projects are recorded as such.  Per-moniker fact files
# named "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" (state) and
# "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}" (project_id) are written via
# buf_write_fact_multi; consumers walk the emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR /
# BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
#
# RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN is never emitted — Mason SA presence IS the
# discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are silently skipped.
#
# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist.
#
# Args: <oauth_access_token>
zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbgp_depot_state_emit: token required"

  # RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is min=0 in the regime (optional for payor-only
  # operations) but required here to enumerate depot projects.
  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" \
    || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set — required for depot_list"

  # Query Cloud Billing API v1: list all projects under the billing account.
  local -r z_billing_list_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects"
  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_billing_list_url}" "${z_token}" "depot_state_billing"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List billing account projects" "depot_state_billing"

  local z_project_count
  z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" '.projectBillingInfo // [] | length') \
    || z_project_count=0

  test "${z_project_count}" -gt 0 || return 0

  # Per-iteration synthesized locals (BCG exception 2 — declare outside, assign inside)
  local z_index=0
  local z_project_id=""
  local z_billing_enabled=""
  local z_sa_list_infix=""
  local z_sa_list_code=""
  local z_sa_count=""
  local z_sa_index=0
  local z_sa_email=""
  local z_mason_email=""
  local z_mason_local=""
  local z_moniker=""
  local z_crm_infix=""
  local z_lifecycle_state=""
  local z_state=""

  while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
    z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].projectId") \
      || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }

    z_billing_enabled=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "depot_state_billing" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].billingEnabled") \
      || z_billing_enabled="false"

    if test "${z_billing_enabled}" != "true"; then
      buc_log_args "Skipping project with billing disabled: ${z_project_id}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    # List service accounts in this project; treat HTTP errors as skip (not fatal).
    z_sa_list_infix="depot_state_sa_${z_index}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" \
      "${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts" \
      "${z_token}" "${z_sa_list_infix}" || true

    z_sa_list_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}") || z_sa_list_code=""
    if test "${z_sa_list_code}" != "200"; then
      buc_log_args "Skipping project (SA list HTTP ${z_sa_list_code}): ${z_project_id}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    z_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
      || z_sa_count=0

    # Scan SA list for a Mason SA — moniker portion enforced to RBRR's
    # ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape so an unrelated SA literally named e.g.
    # "mason-Foo" cannot be misclassified as a depot.
    z_mason_email=""
    z_sa_index=0
    while test "${z_sa_index}" -lt "${z_sa_count}"; do
      z_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_list_infix}" ".accounts[${z_sa_index}].email") \
        || { z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1)); continue; }
      if [[ "${z_sa_email}" =~ ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@[^@]*\.iam\.gserviceaccount\.com$ ]]; then
        z_mason_email="${z_sa_email}"
        break
      fi
      z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1))
    done

    if test -z "${z_mason_email}"; then
      buc_log_args "Skipping project — no Mason SA found: ${z_project_id}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    # Derive moniker from Mason SA local-part (strip leading mason- prefix)
    z_mason_local="${z_mason_email%%@*}"
    z_moniker="${z_mason_local#"${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-"}"

    # Determine lifecycle state via CRM v1.
    z_crm_infix="depot_state_crm_${z_index}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" \
      "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}" \
      "${z_token}" "${z_crm_infix}" || true

    z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_crm_infix}" '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"') \
      || z_lifecycle_state="UNKNOWN"

    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
      z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
    elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
      z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
    else
      buc_log_args "Skipping moniker ${z_moniker} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      continue
    fi

    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" "${z_state}"
    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}" "${z_project_id}"

    z_index=$((z_index + 1))
  done
}
input.new_string:
# Probe per-installation depot states and emit per-moniker fact files.
# Classification is by Mason SA presence — any billing-account project hosting
# a service account matching ^mason-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@ is a depot.  The moniker
# is derived from the Mason SA local-part after stripping the mason- prefix.
# Lifecycle is determined first via CRM v1; only ACTIVE and DELETE_REQUESTED
# projects are candidates for SA-list scanning.  Per-moniker fact files
# named "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}" (state) and
# "<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}" (project_id) are written via
# buf_write_fact_multi; consumers walk the emitted files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR /
# BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem is the data bus).
#
# Mason SA presence IS the discriminator; projects without a Mason SA are
# silently skipped (no BROKEN state emitted).
#
# Billing v1 projects list and IAM v1 serviceAccounts list are paginated via
# nextPageToken; per-page infixes keep response files distinct.
#
# Single-shot per dispatch — buf_write_fact_multi dies on preexist.
#
# Args: <oauth_access_token>
zrbgp_depot_state_emit() {
  zrbgp_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbgp_depot_state_emit: token required"

  # RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is min=0 in the regime (optional for payor-only
  # operations) but required here to enumerate depot projects.
  test -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" \
    || buc_die "RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID is not set — required for depot_list"

  local -r z_billing_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING}${RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1}/billingAccounts/${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}/projects"

  # Per-iteration synthesized locals (BCG exception 2 — declare outside, assign inside)
  local z_billing_page=1
  local z_billing_page_token=""
  local z_billing_url=""
  local z_billing_tok_enc=""
  local z_billing_infix=""
  local z_project_count=0
  local z_index=0
  local z_global_project_index=0
  local z_project_id=""
  local z_crm_infix=""
  local z_crm_code=""
  local z_lifecycle_state=""
  local z_state=""
  local z_sa_url_base=""
  local z_sa_url=""
  local z_sa_page=1
  local z_sa_page_token=""
  local z_sa_tok_enc=""
  local z_sa_infix=""
  local z_sa_list_code=""
  local z_sa_count=0
  local z_sa_index=0
  local z_sa_email=""
  local z_mason_email=""
  local z_mason_local=""
  local z_moniker=""

  while :; do
    z_billing_url="${z_billing_url_base}"
    if test -n "${z_billing_page_token}"; then
      z_billing_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_billing_page_token}") \
        || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode Billing v1 pageToken"
      z_billing_url="${z_billing_url}?pageToken=${z_billing_tok_enc}"
    fi

    z_billing_infix="depot_state_billing_${z_billing_page}"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_billing_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_billing_infix}"
    rbgu_http_require_ok "List billing account projects" "${z_billing_infix}"

    z_project_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" '.projectBillingInfo // [] | length') \
      || z_project_count=0

    z_index=0
    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_project_count}"; do
      z_project_id=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_billing_infix}" ".projectBillingInfo[${z_index}].projectId") \
        || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1)); continue; }

      # CRM v1 lifecycle lookup gates the SA list — cheaper call, single
      # classification decision before potentially-failing IAM scan.
      z_crm_infix="depot_state_crm_${z_global_project_index}"
      rbgu_http_json "GET" \
        "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}" \
        "${z_token}" "${z_crm_infix}" || true

      z_crm_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_crm_infix}") || z_crm_code=""
      if test "${z_crm_code}" != "200"; then
        buc_log_args "Skipping project (CRM lookup HTTP ${z_crm_code}): ${z_project_id}"
        z_index=$((z_index + 1))
        z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
        continue
      fi

      z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_crm_infix}" '.lifecycleState') \
        || z_lifecycle_state=""

      if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED}"
      elif test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
        z_state="${RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE}"
      else
        buc_log_args "Skipping project ${z_project_id} with unrecognized lifecycle state: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
        z_index=$((z_index + 1))
        z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
        continue
      fi

      # Scan SA list for a Mason SA — moniker portion anchored to RBRR's
      # ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$ shape.  Paginated via nextPageToken; break out on
      # first hit since the depot's Mason SA is a singleton.
      z_sa_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/${z_project_id}/serviceAccounts"
      z_sa_page=1
      z_sa_page_token=""
      z_mason_email=""

      while :; do
        z_sa_url="${z_sa_url_base}"
        if test -n "${z_sa_page_token}"; then
          z_sa_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_sa_page_token}") \
            || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode IAM v1 pageToken"
          z_sa_url="${z_sa_url}?pageToken=${z_sa_tok_enc}"
        fi

        z_sa_infix="depot_state_sa_${z_global_project_index}_${z_sa_page}"
        rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_sa_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_sa_infix}" || true

        z_sa_list_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_sa_infix}") || z_sa_list_code=""
        if test "${z_sa_list_code}" != "200"; then
          buc_log_args "Skipping project (SA list HTTP ${z_sa_list_code}): ${z_project_id}"
          break
        fi

        z_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
          || z_sa_count=0

        z_sa_index=0
        while test "${z_sa_index}" -lt "${z_sa_count}"; do
          z_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_sa_infix}" ".accounts[${z_sa_index}].email") \
            || { z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1)); continue; }
          if [[ "${z_sa_email}" =~ ^${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-[a-z][a-z0-9]*@[^@]*\.iam\.gserviceaccount\.com$ ]]; then
            z_mason_email="${z_sa_email}"
            break
          fi
          z_sa_index=$((z_sa_index + 1))
        done

        test -n "${z_mason_email}" && break

        z_sa_page_token=$(jq -r '.nextPageToken // empty' \
          "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_sa_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}") \
          || z_sa_page_token=""
        test -n "${z_sa_page_token}" || break
        z_sa_page=$((z_sa_page + 1))
      done

      if test -z "${z_mason_email}"; then
        buc_log_args "Skipping project — no Mason SA found: ${z_project_id}"
        z_index=$((z_index + 1))
        z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
        continue
      fi

      # Derive moniker from Mason SA local-part (strip leading mason- prefix)
      z_mason_local="${z_mason_email%%@*}"
      z_moniker="${z_mason_local#"${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-"}"

      buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}"         "${z_state}"
      buf_write_fact_multi "${z_moniker}" "${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT}" "${z_project_id}"

      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
      z_global_project_index=$((z_global_project_index + 1))
    done

    z_billing_page_token=$(jq -r '.nextPageToken // empty' \
      "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_billing_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}") \
      || z_billing_page_token=""
    test -n "${z_billing_page_token}" || break
    z_billing_page=$((z_billing_page + 1))
  done
}
